title: Modeling Challenging Patient Interactions: LLMs for Medical Communication Training
citation: Bodonhelyi A, Stegemann-Philipps C, Sonanini A, Herschbach L, Szep M, Herrmann-Werner A, Festl-Wietek T, Kasneci E, Holderried F. Modeling Challenging Patient Interactions: LLMs for Medical Communication Training. arXiv:2503.22250v2. 2025.
permitted_uses: Architecture inspiration for virtual patient communication styles, prompt scaffolds, emotional response QA, multilingual persona support, and difficult-patient scenario authoring.
claims_supported: The preprint describes virtual patients based on Satir communication styles, specifically accuser and rationalizer personas.
The implementation used author notes, first assistant messages, behavioral instruction prompts, hidden thoughts, and a bounded stubbornness mechanism to improve role consistency.
The authors reported that early prompts drifted toward neutral tone after 4 to 6 prompts unless mood and situation fields were made more detailed.
The study used questionnaires, Likert realism/authenticity ratings, adjective identification, emotion analysis, and sentiment analysis to evaluate persona adherence.
The authors cautioned that repeated predefined responses and unbounded nonverbal cue prompting can become unnatural or exaggerated.
claims_not_supported: That the approach is peer-reviewed clinical validation.
That Satir-style virtual patients are sufficient for high-stakes scoring.
That text-only emotion analysis proves embodied XR behavior fidelity.
That all communication styles, specialties, languages, or cultural contexts have been validated.
